content:
if ai disappeared tomorrow, 70% of linkedin would go suspiciously quiet.



last night i was scrolling through linkedin and somewhere between the "i'm humbled to announce..." posts and the ai-generated life lessons, i realised i wasn't scrolling a professional network anymore.

i was watching machines politely talk to each other.

the process is surprisingly simple.

person A opens chatgpt.

"write me a thought leadership post about leadership."

the post gets published under their name.

person b reads it.
copies it.
drops it into claude.

"write me an insightful comment that sounds like i actually read this."

comment posted.

person A gets the notification.

back to chatgpt.

*"reply professionally. sound grateful. add a little authority."*

reply posted.

now two ais have had a meaningful conversation.

the humans were simply responsible for clicking "copy" and "paste."

somewhere in the middle, there's a founder paying thousands for personal branding advice to learn how to build "authentic relationships."

the irony deserves its own ted talk.

and somehow, everyone walks away feeling productive.

someone copies an idea from a creator in India or US.

someone else rewrites it with ai.

another person asks ai to make it "more emotional."

someone turns it into a carousel.

a week later, the same idea has travelled across continents, through half a dozen prompts, and into the feeds of people calling it an original thought.

then we proudly say, "i created content today."

did we?

or did we just become middle managers between one chatbot and another?

what a spectacular time to be building a "personal" brand.

like seriously???? 

let me know.. am I right or wrong?

~ Priyanka ❤️
